title: play kube: invalid character 'N' looking for beginning of value
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`play kube` error in latest HEAD

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
cat <<EOF >manifest.yml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx:1.14.2
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
EOF
```

1. `podman play kube ./manifest.yml`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: ./manifest.yml: invalid character 'N' looking for beginning of value
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

On stable version (tested tag `v4.1.1`) I get this error instead: 

```
Error: ./manifest.yml: error playing YAML file: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0-dev
API Version:  4.2.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Git Commit:   a298909decc9091da8cc134e6eb2f38a70635a30
Built:        Tue Jul 26 12:08:11 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 22 17:17:44 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
```
apiVersion: v1$
kind: Pod$
metadata:$
  name: nginx$
spec:$
  containers:$
  - name: nginx$
    image: nginx:1.14.2$
    ports:$
    - containerPort: 80$
```

Copy-pasted from https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/

The issue occurs when a new client talks to an older server.  I think I know what's going on.

@nicrowe00, I fear we need to let the remote client to use the `play/kube` endpoint.  Otherwise, we run into this issue.  Would you open a PR to fix it?
Nice catch, @1player! Thanks for the report.

~~@mheon @jwhonce @Luap99 how have we historically dealt with such issues.  Assuming we add an entirely new command, how do newer clients negotiate with older server of the same major version, for instance, a v4.2 client with a v4.1 server?
Did we just hope for the best?~~

Scratch that.  I took some time to think this through.  New commands naturally won't work.

Old commands must continue working so we should just continue using the `play/kube` endpoint as outlined above.

Yes, the client should use the old endpoint for backwards compat. Although I am confused about the current error message I would expect a 404 not found when calling non exiting endpoints.
Alternatively you could make a version check in the bindings but since this is the same endpoint just with different paths there is really no reason to do so.
